article.body_preview:
DHAKA - When people are already struggling to cope with high prices of daily essentials, a fresh hike in the prices of soybean oil, sugar, onions, and potatoes has dealt them a further blow.

This will only force them to dig deeper into their pockets at a time when inflation is close to double digits.

Crude soybean oil refiners on Wednesday raised the price of one-litre bottled soybean oil by Tk 12 to Tk 199 while the price of loose soybean oil was increased by Tk 9 to Tk 176 a litre. A consume

llm_connections.evidence_quote: Bangladesh Vegetable Oil Refiners' and Vanaspati Manufacturers' Association hiked the prices after consulting with the commerce ministry and the Bangladesh Trade and Tariff Commission.
llm_connections.scope_justification: The sentence explicitly attributes a decision by the vegetable-oil refiners to hike prices, which directly affects the specific product (soybean oil) discussed earlier in the article.

llm_connections.evidence_quote: they have already cut consumption of desserts like payesh and shemai because of the high prices of sugar.
llm_connections.scope_justification: The quote explicitly says consumers cut weekly consumption of sugar-based desserts due to high sugar prices, directly affecting weekly sales performance consistent with Sales_lag_7's weekly scope.
